bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s offer is valid but gives me the lowest value on every issue, so I treat the “balanced/win-win” message as cheap talk contradicted by the numbers.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.98) because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated an all-valueE offer, which gives me very low utility, and their “balanced” messages are not supported by the numbers. To avoid simply repeating my prior bid, I am making a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the highest-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.98 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly from an all-valueE offer to improving issueA, which raises my utility only from about 0.20 to 0.26, so their “balanced” messages are not supported by the numbers. I will make a small concession on a lower-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little reason to concede on the high-weight issues A, B, and C. The opponent has only made a small concession on issueA while keeping the rest at my worst values, so I am treating their “balanced” messages as cheap talk and conceding only on my lowest-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me despite small moves on issueA and issueB. Their messages frame the offers as fair, but the actual bids are still mostly valueE, so I will make only limited concessions on my lowest-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me, mostly on issueA or issueB while keeping the rest at my worst values, so I will concede only on low-weight issues and hold firm on the most important issues.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.90 because it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has made only small concessions and their offers remain low-value to me, despite their “win-win” language, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding slightly on a low-weight issue to keep movement going.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high utility around 0.90 while making a visible concession on lower-weight issues rather than giving up value on issueA, issueB, or issueC. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly for me but remain very low-value overall, and their “balanced/win-win” messages are not supported by the actual bids. I will avoid repeating my last exact bid by shifting concessions across issueD and issueE.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly for me, especially on issueA and occasionally issueB, but they still keep most issues at valueE; I will concede mainly on lower-weight issues D and E while holding firm on high-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 while making a visible concession from my last offer. The opponent’s messages claim balance, but their offers show they strongly hold issueD and issueE at valueE while only modestly moving on issueA/B/C, so I concede first on the lower-weight issueD and keep my high-weight issueA/issueB/issueC at valueA.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84 because it is early at t=0.20, so I should not concede much on the high-weight issues A and B. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly for me, with their latest bid worth about 0.44, and their “balanced/win-win” messages are not enough to justify major concessions. I will concede fully on the two lowest-weight issues, D and E, while holding firm on A, B, and C.

At t=0.22 we are still early enough to target a high-utility bid, while making a modest concession beyond my prior position to avoid deadlock. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their “fair/win-win” messages are not supported by the numbers, though they have shown occasional movement on issueA/issueB; I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues and concede mainly on lower/medium-weight issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 because it is early-to-mid negotiation time, so I should concede gradually without giving up the high-weight issueA and issueB positions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueB and issueC, but their “win-win” messages are cheap talk and their bids remain much lower utility to me than my current target. I am therefore making a real concession on issueC while keeping low-weight issueD and issueE at their preferred side.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while conceding further on issueC and leaving the lower-weight issues D/E at valueE. At t=0.26 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA and issueC, so a measured concession is appropriate. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual offers still mostly favor low-value outcomes for me, so I am not conceding on both top-weight issues yet.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the most important issues, issueA and issueB. At t=0.28 there is still enough time not to chase the opponent’s very low offers, and their history shows only small, scattered concessions despite repeated “balanced” messaging, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issueC/issueD/issueE rather than on my top priorities.

I am targeting about 0.66 utility: still safely above reservation and not too generous at t=0.30, but more conciliatory than my prior high-utility bids. The opponent’s offers have improved only sporadically for me, with their best around 0.48, and their “balanced/win-win” messages are not enough to justify accepting a low package. I am holding firm on issueA while conceding some on issueB and issueC, where the history suggests they may value movement.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.62, which is still clearly above reservation while moving closer to values the opponent has repeatedly signaled. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure but not enough to fully accept their low-utility offers, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueA while conceding on lower-weight issues and moving issueB/issueC toward their recent proposals. Their messages claim fairness, but the actual offers show gradual concessions mainly on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so I trust the bidding pattern over the language.

I am targeting a moderate utility of about 0.58: enough concession to move toward agreement, but still above the opponent’s recent offers to me. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their very low packages; their bidding shows willingness to give on issueA and recently issueC, while their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, so I concede low-weight issueD/issueE and partially on issueB.

I am targeting about 0.72 utility by holding firm on the two highest-weight issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding substantially on lower-weight issueD and issueE. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest offer improved my utility and shows willingness to move on issueA/issueB, so I am countering with a package that combines concessions they have already shown separately rather than accepting their framing as fully “balanced.”

I am targeting about 0.66 utility: a real concession from my earlier high-utility bids, while still protecting issueA and issueB because they carry the most weight for me. At t=0.38 there is moderate time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest low-value package; their offers have fluctuated, with one meaningful signal around issueA/issueB, while their “balanced” messages are not reliable beyond the actual bids.

I am targeting about 0.68 utility by holding firm on the two most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding the lower/mid-weight issues C, D, and E. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s poorer offers; their bids show willingness to concede either issueA or issueB at times, while their “win-win” messages are not binding and are less informative than the actual offer history.
